key_people.name: Kim Jae-yeon
key_people.title: Standing Leader (상임대표)
key_people.title_description: Party leader responsible for overall party strategy and direction
key_people.description: Served as 19th National Assembly member via Unified Progressive Party proportional representation. Became Progressive Party standing leader in June 2020, re-elected in June 2024. 2025 presidential election candidate.

key_people.name: Yoon Jong-oh
key_people.title: Floor Leader (원내대표)
key_people.title_description: Leads the party caucus in the National Assembly
key_people.description: Elected from Ulsan Buk-gu district in the 22nd National Assembly. Previously served as 20th National Assembly member.

key_people.name: Jeon Jong-deok
key_people.title: National Assembly Member (국회의원)
key_people.title_description: Proportional representation member via Democratic Alliance of Korea
key_people.description: Elected in the 2024 legislative election through the Democratic Alliance coalition.

key_people.name: Jeong Hye-kyung
key_people.title: National Assembly Member (국회의원)
key_people.title_description: Proportional representation member via Democratic Alliance of Korea
key_people.description: Elected in the 2024 legislative election through the Democratic Alliance coalition.

key_people.name: Son Sol
key_people.title: National Assembly Member (국회의원)
key_people.title_description: Inherited proportional representation seat, youngest member of 22nd National Assembly
key_people.description: Born 1995. Former Ewha Womans University student council president and party chief spokesperson. Succeeded a Democratic Alliance proportional representation seat in June 2025, becoming the youngest member of the 22nd National Assembly at age 30.

recent_events.title: Progressive Party wins 3 seats in 22nd National Assembly election
recent_events.description: The party joined the Democratic Alliance of Korea coalition led by the Democratic Party and secured 3 proportional representation seats (Yoon Jong-oh, Jeon Jong-deok, Jeong Hye-kyung) in the April 2024 legislative election.
recent_events.impact: Returned the Progressive Party to parliamentary presence after losing all seats in 2020

recent_events.title: Kim Jae-yeon re-elected as standing leader
recent_events.description: Kim Jae-yeon was re-elected as the Progressive Party's standing leader for a new term at the party convention on June 14, 2024.
recent_events.impact: Continuity of leadership as the party re-entered parliament

recent_events.title: Hong Seong-gyu announces bid for Gyeonggi Governor
recent_events.description: Senior Spokesperson Hong Seong-gyu declared candidacy for Gyeonggi Province Governor in the upcoming June 3 local elections, marking the party's expansion into executive races.
recent_events.impact: Signals party ambition beyond legislative representation

recent_events.title: Son Sol becomes 4th Progressive Party National Assembly member
recent_events.description: Son Sol, former party chief spokesperson, inherited a Democratic Alliance proportional representation seat and became the youngest member of the 22nd National Assembly at age 30, bringing the Progressive Party's total to 4 seats.
recent_events.impact: Expanded party's legislative presence from 3 to 4 seats

recent_events.title: Kim Jae-yeon proposes 5-party progressive electoral alliance
recent_events.description: Standing Leader Kim Jae-yeon proposed a democratic-reform-progressive 5-party electoral alliance to counter the conservative bloc, calling for coalition talks among progressive parties ahead of the June local elections and by-elections.

related_entities.org_description: Main center-left ruling party; led the Democratic Alliance of Korea coalition with the Progressive Party in 2024
Progressive party founded by Cho Kuk; fellow opposition party

related_entities.org_name: National Assembly of the Republic of Korea
related_entities.org_description: National legislature where the Progressive Party holds 4 seats
Main conservative party; primary political rival

core_business: 韩国进步党（진보당）是韩国左翼进步主义政党，2017年10月由新民众党和民众联合党合并成立，初名民众党（민중당），2020年6月更名为进步党。该党意识形态为进步主义、左翼民族主义和反帝国主义，主张解散财阀垄断经济、财富再分配（包括对30亿韩元以上遗产征收90%税率）、反对美韩军事同盟、支持朝韩和平统一和阳光政策、废除征兵制建立志愿军队。社会政策方面积极支持女权运动、LGBT权利和劳动者权益，是韩国政坛少数公开支持女权主义的政党。2024年国会选举中通过加入共同民主党主导的民主联合获得3个比例代表议席，2025年6月增至4席。现任常任代表为金在妍（Kim Jae-yeon），院内代表为尹钟悟（Yoon Jong-oh）。2026年目标为地方选举中产出150名以上当选人。
apec_stance: 韩国进步党在对外经济政策上持保护主义立场，反对跨国资本垄断，主张韩国在经济外交中保持独立自主。该党反对新自由主义全球化，对APEC等自由贸易框架持批判态度，认为其服务于大国利益。在2025年韩国主办APEC期间，进步党关注焦点在于防止自由贸易协定损害韩国劳动者和农业利益，主张优先保护本国产业和劳动者权益。该党反对向乌克兰提供致命性武器援助，认为尹锡悦政府的外交政策过度倾向美国而损害了与俄罗斯的关系。总体而言，进步党对APEC持谨慎甚至批评立场，更强调经济主权和劳动者保护而非贸易自由化。

collection_meta.notes: Enriched profile: fixed incorrect Brazilian Progressistas data with correct Korean Progressive Party (진보당) information. Added key people, recent events, related entities, core business, and APEC stance.

quotes.title: Progressive Party's Hong Seong-gyu Gyeonggi Governor Bid - Chosun
